conflictBlocks.oursContent: // Note: some browsers require a user gesture. We'll attempt anyway.
conflictBlocks.theirsContent:
// A warmer, more "premium" bell-like chime.
  // Note: some browsers require a user gesture before audio can start; this is best-effort.
const now = ctx.currentTime;

oscA.frequency.setValueAtTime(frequency, startTime);
    oscB.frequency.setValueAtTime(frequency * 1.006, startTime);

conflictBlocks.oursContent: const now = ctx.currentTime;

const colors = ["#22C55E", "#3B82F6", "#F59E0B", "#EF4444", "#A855F7"];
    const count = 220;

rotStart,
        rotEnd,
        blur: isSparkle ? 0.5 : 0,
        glow: isSparkle ? 10 : 0,
conflictBlocks.theirsContent:
startY,
        isSparkle,

conflictBlocks.oursContent: const durationMs = 1550;
conflictBlocks.theirsContent: const durationMs = 1700;

conflictBlocks.oursContent: const eased = easeOutExpo(t);
conflictBlocks.theirsContent: const eased = t < 0.86 ? easeOutBack(t / 0.86) : 1;

conflictBlocks.oursContent:
{prefersReducedMotion ? null : (
        <div className="absolute inset-0 overflow-hidden pointer-events-none">
          {confettiPieces.map((p) => {
            const style = {
              left: `${p.left}%`,
              width: `${p.w}px`,
              height: `${p.h}px`,
              borderRadius: `${p.radius}px`,
              backgroundColor: p.color,
              opacity: p.opacity,
              animationDelay: `${p.delay}s`,
              animationDuration: `${p.duration}s`,
              "--confetti-drift": `${p.drift}px`,
              "--confetti-rot-start": `${p.rotStart}deg`,
              "--confetti-rot-end": `${p.rotEnd}deg`,
              "--confetti-blur": `${p.blur}px`,
              "--confetti-glow": `${p.glow}px`,
            };
conflictBlocks.theirsContent:
<div className="absolute inset-0 overflow-hidden pointer-events-none">
        {confettiPieces.map((p) => {
          const heightPx = Math.max(6, p.size * 0.55);
          const baseStyle = {
            left: `${p.left}%`,
            width: `${p.size}px`,
            height: `${heightPx}px`,
            backgroundColor: p.color,
            animationDelay: `${p.delay}s`,
            animationDuration: `${p.duration}s`,
            transform: `rotate(${p.rotate}deg)`,
            "--confetti-drift": `${p.drift}px`,
            "--confetti-start-y": `${p.startY}vh`,
          };

conflictBlocks.oursContent: transform: translate3d(0, -18vh, 0) rotate(var(--confetti-rot-start));
conflictBlocks.theirsContent: transform: translate3d(0, var(--confetti-start-y), 0) rotate(0deg);

conflictBlocks.oursContent:
transform: translate3d(var(--confetti-drift), 112vh, 0)
              rotate(var(--confetti-rot-end));
animation-timing-function: cubic-bezier(0.18, 0.8, 0.2, 1);
conflictBlocks.theirsContent: animation-timing-function: cubic-bezier(0.14, 0.72, 0.18, 1);
filter: drop-shadow(0 6px 12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.28));

conflictBlocks.oursContent:
animation: celebrateNumberPulse 520ms ease-out;
          text-shadow: 0 12px 28px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
conflictBlocks.theirsContent:
animation: celebrateNumberPulse 520ms ease-out;
          text-shadow: 0 12px 26px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.14);

preservedBehaviors: exports a default CelebrationOverlay function component with 'use client' directive
imports React hooks (useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState) and formatGBP utility
uses AudioContext with webkitAudioContext fallback for cross-browser audio
confettiPieces generated via useMemo with Array.from for stable confetti on mount
requestAnimationFrame-based revenue counter animation with easing
auto-close setTimeout calling onDone after delay
celebrate-confetti and celebrateNumberPulse CSS keyframe animations
isSoundEnabled state for mute/unmute toggle with Volume2/VolumeX icons
close button and backdrop with click-to-close and aria-labels
safeRevenueBefore, safeRevenueAfter, safeAddedMonthlyFee computed with Number.isFinite
yearly run-rate display (monthly * 12)
cleanup of animation frame and timeout on unmount (cancelAnimationFrame + clearTimeout)
startedRef guard to prevent double-play in React strict mode
ctx.close() called in a setTimeout for audio cleanup
chime function accepts volume parameter with clamping (Math.min/Math.max)
chime function accepts onAutoplayBlocked callback for autoplay detection
ctx.resume() called to handle browser autoplay restrictions
context suspension check (ctx.state !== 'running') to detect blocked autoplay
onAutoplayBlocked callback invoked when context stays suspended
chime returns result object with ok boolean and reason string
returns { ok: false, reason: 'no-window' } when window is undefined
returns { ok: false, reason: 'no-audio-context' } when AudioContext is missing
safeClose helper function for delayed context closing
confetti shape system producing w, h, radius (thin rectangle, square, ribbon)
confetti pieces have rotStart and rotEnd for per-piece rotation
CSS uses --confetti-rot-start and --confetti-rot-end custom properties
prefersReducedMotion check conditionally hides confetti (renders null)
CSS @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) hides confetti and disables animation
confetti blur effect via custom property
confetti glow effect via box-shadow or custom property
try/catch with 'Failed to play chime' error logging in chime function
returns { ok: false, reason: 'exception' } on chime error
soundBlocked state for blocked autoplay UI with 'Play sound' retry button
confetti opacity varies for sparkle vs normal pieces
easeOutBack function with overshoot constant 1.70158
easeOutBack used in tick with 0.86 threshold for animation easing
DynamicsCompressor node created in audio chain
delay node created for room/reverb simulation
feedback gain node in delay feedback loop
lowpass biquad filter (damp) in delay feedback loop
wet and dry signal paths in audio routing
compressor connects to master gain which connects to ctx.destination
expanded color palette includes pink (#EC4899)
at least 5 distinct colors in confetti palette
confetti pieces have startY property for variable start position above screen
CSS animation uses --confetti-start-y custom property
isSparkle determined by modulo 9 on piece index
celebrate-confetti-sparkle CSS class for sparkle confetti pieces
sparkle CSS class has box-shadow for glow effect
sparkle CSS class uses mix-blend-mode: screen
auto-close timer set to 3800ms
animation duration set to 1700ms
confetti animation uses drop-shadow filter
multiple oscillators with detune for rich chime sound
low-frequency tap/thump oscillator at chime start
confetti drift using (Math.random() - 0.5) for positive and negative spread
confetti count is at least 180 pieces
